evidence.metricsTable:
| Approach | Config | Scale | Bias | MSE | RMSE | Relative Bias | CI Coverage |
| Single-stage | C2 | 10MB | -0.0287 | 0.0168 | 0.1297 | 0.0475 | 0.02 |
| Two-stage | C2 | 10MB | -0.0099 | 0.0008 | 0.0279 | -0.0168 | 0.76 |
keyFindings: Two-stage RMSE 4.6x better at 10MB, 8.9x better at 100MB
Both approaches converge in 4 IRLS iterations

assertions.title: Report includes memory and scaling analysis

steps.expected: Rust vs R peak memory comparison with quantitative data
steps.observed: Section 4 'Memory and Scaling Analysis' with subsection 'Memory Efficiency: Fused Kernel vs Lag Matrix Materialization'

steps.expected: Memory values comparing Rust vs R
steps.observed: R baseline: 2.0 GB per column (10 GB total for C2 at 1GB), Rust: 1.8 GB output matrix only, ~5x reduction in temporary allocations
Scaling behavior table with R baseline vs Rust scaling (10MB→100MB: R 10.0x, Rust 10.3x; 100MB→1GB: R 19.5x, Rust 11.7x; 10MB→1GB: R 194.7x, Rust 121.3x)

evidence.memorySection: ### Memory Efficiency: Fused Kernel vs Lag Matrix Materialization

memoryValues.rBaseline: 2.0 GB per column, up to 10 GB total for C2

evidence.keyFinding: R baseline shows super-linear scaling (194.7x for 100x data), Rust shows near-linear scaling (121.3x)

summary: Tested 5 assertions: all 5 passed. The report directory contains 5 PNG plots (speedup_comparison.png, convergence_bias.png, ci_coverage.png, scaling_curves.png, memory_scaling.png) exceeding the minimum requirement of 4. The markdown report (25KB) contains all required sections including Introduction, Optimization Methodology, Benchmark Results, Memory and Scaling Analysis, Mixmeta Comparison, Convergence Analysis, and Conclusions. Multiple benchmark comparison tables with pipe-separated formatting show R vs Rust timing. The Convergence Analysis section includes detailed quantitative metrics (Bias, MSE, RMSE, CI Coverage) with interpretation comparing single-stage vs two-stage mixmeta. The Memory and Scaling Analysis section includes quantitative memory comparisons (~5x reduction) and scaling behavior data.
